comment: Example configurations for AEM Quality Gate workflow

example_1_single_repo_safe_mode.description: Analyze a single AEM repository with explicit approval gates
notes: Safe Mode (default) — you'll see permission gates for setup
Scanning and reporting run automatically (no gates)
Reports saved to <baseDir>/<repoName>/quality-reports/
Duration: ~5-10 minutes depending on project size

example_2_multiple_repos_trusted_mode.description: Analyze multiple repositories in fully automated mode
notes: Trusted Mode — zero approval gates, fully automated
All 3 repos scanned in parallel where possible
Perfect for CI/CD pipelines and scheduled batch runs
Returns aggregated quality ratings for all repos

example_3_with_ai_curation.description: Analyze project and propose AI-driven rule tuning
notes: Safe Mode + AI Curation enabled
After scanning, AI reads the aggregated report and proposes:
- Rule severity adjustments (e.g., demote noisy rules)
- Suppressions for false positives
- New rule candidates for uncovered patterns
You review and approve/deny the proposals
Approved changes update rules-manifest.json for future scans
No code re-analysis by AI — only rule definition optimization

example_4_custom_location_ci_cd.description: CI/CD pipeline with custom repository location
example_4_custom_location_ci_cd.ciCode:
.github/workflows/quality-gate.yml:
  - name: Run AEM Quality Gate
    run: |
      /aem-quality-gate --args '{
        "trustedMode": true,
        "baseDir": "/runner/quality",
        "repositories": [{
          "repoUrl": "${{ github.repository }}",
          "repoName": "${GITHUB_REF##*/}",
          "branch": "${{ github.ref_name }}"
        }]
      }'

  - name: Upload Quality Reports
    uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
    with:
      name: quality-gate-reports
      path: /runner/quality/**/quality-reports/
notes: baseDir defaults to $HOME/Documents/project-source/project-unit-test cases/repos
Use custom baseDir for CI/CD runners where default path doesn't exist
All reports stored in /ci/aem-quality-reports/
Reports can be uploaded as GitHub artifacts or archived

example_5_comprehensive_governance.description: Comprehensive quality governance: org-wide scanning with curation

postScan.step3: Quarterly: run with aiCuration: true to propose rule updates
postScan.step4: Report to stakeholders: which dimensions are improving/regressing

notes: Scans 4 major repos in parallel
Stores historical reports for trend analysis
Identify which teams/repos have best quality culture
Use AI curation quarterly, not on every scan
Reports shared with architecture and security teams

example_6_pr_validation.description: Pull request validation — test quality before merge
notes: Safe Mode (user must approve setup) — or use trustedMode for full automation
Runs quality gate on each PR
Comments results directly on PR for visibility
Developers see quality impact before merge
Not blocking (soft gate) — allows merge, but flags issues

example_7_dev_local_workflow.description: Developer local workflow — quick feedback during development
example_7_dev_local_workflow.usage:
1. Developer is working on feature/component-x locally
2. Run: /aem-quality-gate --args '{...}'
3. Quality Gate clones from local path, scans, reports
4. Developer reviews findings in quality-reports/ directory
5. Fixes blocker/critical issues before pushing
6. Re-runs quality gate to confirm improvements
notes: repoUrl can be a local file:// path for testing without network
Safe Mode gives developer time to review before auto-scanning
Reports help identify issues early, before code review
Much faster iteration than waiting for CI/CD feedback

example_8_gradual_enforcement.description: Gradual rule enforcement — start permissive, tighten over time
example_8_gradual_enforcement.scenario: Organization wants to adopt quality gates but has legacy code with 1000+ issues
approach: Month 1: Run quality gate, gather baseline metrics (all rules enabled)
Month 1-2: Disable rules that are 50%+ false positives via rules-manifest.json
Month 2-3: Address blocker/critical findings in backlog
Month 3-4: Use AI curation to propose new rules
Month 4+: Gradually enforce: promote rules from 'info' → 'minor' → 'major'
notes: Don't enable all rules at once on legacy code — too much noise, low adoption
Start permissive, gradually tighten — gives teams time to improve
Use AI curation monthly to refine rule set based on actual findings
Track per-dimension ratings monthly to show progress to leadership
